methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $2.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Atmo is a Series A–stage AI & Software company founded by Alex Levy and Johan Mathe that has raised $15M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR), including same-stage AI software businesses such as Liquid AI, Field AI, and Twelve Labs.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $15M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $30M. Peer-median analysis identifies three same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $2.0B and a range from $250M (Twelve Labs) to $2.4B (Liquid AI). This method implies $2.0B.

The triangulated analysis produces a wide range: $26M at the low end (stage-progression floor), $1.0B at the median, and $2.3B at the high end (peer maximum). The substantial spread reflects material uncertainty inherent in early-stage AI valuations, where peer multiples vary significantly. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively to realized capital; peer multiples anchor to market pricing but exhibit high dispersion. Key uncertainties include Atmo's revenue traction relative to comparables, product differentiation within a crowded frontier-AI sector, and whether peer valuations themselves reflect sustainable fundamentals or sector exuberance.
